  local function checkQ (v)
    local s = string.format("%q", v)
    local nv = load("return " .. s)()
    assert(v == nv and math.type(v) == math.type(nv))
  end
  checkQ("\0\0\1\255\u{234}")
  checkQ(math.maxinteger)
  checkQ(math.mininteger)
  checkQ(math.pi)
  checkQ(0.1)
  checkQ(true)
  checkQ(nil)
  checkQ(false)
  checkQ(math.huge)
  checkQ(-math.huge)
  assert(string.format("%q", 0/0) == "(0/0)")   -- NaN
  checkerror("no literal", string.format, "%q", {})
end

input.description: Look at the official test runner

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

content.content:
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then

content.text:
Fix verified for `math.maxinteger`. The integer is now formatted as a decimal integer literal and round-trips correctly.

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1533` — the `%q` handler used `state.get_at(arg)` to discriminate integer vs float, but `state.get_at(i32)` treats the i32 as an *absolute* stack index (via `StackIdxConv::From<i32>`), while every other accessor in `addliteral` (`state.type_at`, `state.to_integer`, `state.to_number`) is CI-relative. With an absolute index of 1/2, the wrong slot was inspected, the `LuaValue::Int` match always failed, and integers like `math.maxinteger` / `math.mininteger` fell through to the float branch — producing `0x1p+63` instead of `9223372036854775807`. Round-tripping that through `load` yields a float, so `math.type(v) == math.type(nv)` failed at strings.lua:231.

**Fix.** Replaced `matches!(state.get_at(arg), LuaValue::Int(_))` with `state.is_integer(arg)`, which routes through `lua_vm::api::is_integer` → `index_to_value` → proper CI-relative resolution.

**New state.** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Direct spot-test confirms `string.format("%q", math.maxinteger)` now yields `9223372036854775807` and round-trips with `math.type` preserved. Did not re-run the full strings.lua suite — mega-loop will re-test.

result:
Fix verified for `math.maxinteger`. The integer is now formatted as a decimal integer literal and round-trips correctly.

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1533` — the `%q` handler used `state.get_at(arg)` to discriminate integer vs float, but `state.get_at(i32)` treats the i32 as an *absolute* stack index (via `StackIdxConv::From<i32>`), while every other accessor in `addliteral` (`state.type_at`, `state.to_integer`, `state.to_number`) is CI-relative. With an absolute index of 1/2, the wrong slot was inspected, the `LuaValue::Int` match always failed, and integers like `math.maxinteger` / `math.mininteger` fell through to the float branch — producing `0x1p+63` instead of `9223372036854775807`. Round-tripping that through `load` yields a float, so `math.type(v) == math.type(nv)` failed at strings.lua:231.

**Fix.** Replaced `matches!(state.get_at(arg), LuaValue::Int(_))` with `state.is_integer(arg)`, which routes through `lua_vm::api::is_integer` → `index_to_value` → proper CI-relative resolution.

**New state.** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Direct spot-test confirms `string.format("%q", math.maxinteger)` now yields `9223372036854775807` and round-trips with `math.type` preserved. Did not re-run the full strings.lua suite — mega-loop will re-test.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/qtest.lua <<'EOF'
local function checkQ(v)
  local s = string.format("%q", v)
  print("q for", v, "=>", s)
  local nv = load("return " .. s)()
  print("  loaded back:", nv, "match=", v == nv, "type", math.type(v), math.type(nv))
end
checkQ("\0\0\1\255\u{234}")
checkQ(math.maxinteger)
checkQ(math.mininteger)
checkQ(math.pi)
checkQ(0.1)
checkQ(true)
checkQ(nil)
checkQ(false)
checkQ(math.huge)
checkQ(-math.huge)
print("nan:", string.format("%q", 0/0))
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/qtest.lua 2>&1 | head -40
